109492.explanation: At our initiative, a motion called 'right to challenge' was adopted years ago, enabling residents to take over a municipal task if they believe they can do it better and/or cheaper.

109494.explanation: Yes, under the right conditions, it can actually be of added value to strengthen the rural area, as long as it does not conflict with other (already existing) businesses.

109495.explanation: Good initiatives around energy are welcome to achieve climate goals. The municipality must actively contribute when initiatives come from society or businesses.

109497.explanation: Given the dispersion law, a suitable location was sought but not found. It should only be established if imposed; otherwise, in our view, it can only be discussed if all conditions previously set by the municipal council are met.

109501.explanation: Asten focuses on being a hospitable municipality, where hospitality entrepreneurs also have space to respond in autumn and winter.

109502.explanation: If the national fireworks ban is enacted, it is good to think about alternatives.

109504.explanation: Preventing hearing damage is in everyone's interest.

109507.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate in society, especially children. Children must not suffer because of their family's financial situation.

109509.explanation: We already do this and will continue to do so. In recent years, two new schools have been built and a third is planned. Additionally, two schools have been renovated or expanded. Primary education is important for the future of our village, and therefore we invest in it.

109510.explanation: Every child should have the opportunity to do what they want: music lessons or sports. Music education is already offered by the schools themselves and there is a subsidy for extra music lessons.

109511.explanation: Every club plays an important role in our local community. If a club is struggling, the municipality should always take this seriously and think along about solutions.

109513.explanation: Safety is a core task of the government.

109514.explanation: Especially in unsafe places or in the center.

109521.explanation: Everyone should contribute to society according to their ability. Doing something in return for benefits is not only fair, but it also helps recipients gain contacts and (work) experience.

109523.explanation: Do not increase, keep it as it is. This has been coordinated with the region, so it is fair for everyone in the Peel region.

109525.explanation: We will also do that; it fits this time in which electric driving is becoming more common.

109526.explanation: We will do that; it was recently decided by the municipal council.

109527.explanation: That is already the case. Care providers must be contracted/approved to ensure that care funds are well spent.

109528.explanation: The goal is to find the right price-quality ratio. The fact is that youth care costs are rising sharply, so it is also important to watch the price to keep it affordable.

109530.explanation: The dog tax is actually used to cover the costs of cleaning up dog waste: walking areas, trash bins, etc. Without the dog tax, these costs would be passed on to all residents, dog owners and non-dog owners alike.

109532.explanation: There is high demand for additional business space from local entrepreneurs. We want to meet that demand with a new industrial area.

109534.explanation: There is already a large housing shortage. We treat everyone equally.

109539.explanation: Children must learn to swim and, moreover, it is good for children to exercise.

109541.explanation: Prevention really adds nothing to the municipality of Asten. It is a facility you find in the city.

109543.explanation: Not an end in itself. Apartment complexes exist, but must fit the scale of Asten. Asten is and remains a village, and residential towers do not belong here.

109544.explanation: Under the right conditions, it is good to explore whether this option can also help reduce the housing shortage.

109546.explanation: The center of Asten must remain easily accessible by car, especially near the supermarkets.

109547.explanation: The market must be car-free, except for disabled persons, emergency services, and suppliers for shops.

109549.explanation: We have paid before and now it is good that investments are made in our mobility: A67, N279, and public transport (bus line 320).

109550.explanation: Too general in formulation. We follow national laws and regulations in this area.

109492.explanation: For, by, and with Asten; that way projects have better support.

109494.explanation: The municipality should always allow farmers to open a shop or campsite, but it must suit the business and surroundings and be small-scale.

109495.explanation: Residents can be supported with advice, but not financially.

109497.explanation: In principle, the task is set by the national Distribution Act, which we as a municipality must comply with. The asylum seekers' center must fit in the area and gain support from the surroundings.

109502.explanation: We believe the organization of this fits better with (neighborhood) associations.

109504.explanation: The current standards are sufficient. Nevertheless, discuss this in preliminary consultations and point out hearing protection options for visitors.

109509.explanation: Many schools have already been renovated recently.

109511.explanation: In principle, the associations are privatized, but that does not mean the municipality cannot contribute in cases of urgency.

109514.explanation: The numerous doorbell cameras are sufficient.

109521.explanation: If possible, this can be implemented, but it is not an obligation. Not everyone can comply, and it requires disproportionate capacity.

109525.explanation: It must be possible to park non-electric vehicles in parking spots with charging stations.

109526.explanation: 30 km/h in the center is already included in the traffic plan. Not necessary for the entire built-up area. Look where it is needed and feasible.

109528.explanation: The quality of a service is also (heavily) weighted. Employing a cheaper care provider for longer sometimes costs more than a qualitatively better and relatively more expensive provider who delivers a better result in less time.

109530.explanation: The amount collected via the dog tax is almost equal to the costs made to collect the tax and maintain facilities. These costs would otherwise have to be charged to all residents. We do not find this fair; the polluter pays now.

109532.explanation: The focus is now on a regional business park so local companies can move on and create space for other entrepreneurs. It is important to revitalize existing business parks and look for opportunities for expansion at the edges.

109534.explanation: This only applies to the numbers under the national legal task.

109541.explanation: Together for Asten believes that such a request, like all requests, should be assessed. Does it fit the location, position, and function in Asten? Not an automatic yes or no.

109543.explanation: Does not fit the village character we promote.

109546.explanation: There is already sufficient parking in Asten.

109547.explanation: We support a car-reduced Market, but not a car-free center.

109550.explanation: We follow nationally recognized studies and guidelines; as a small municipality, you cannot determine this yourself.

109492.explanation: Municipal task. If people want to do it for free: fine.

109494.explanation: Incorporate through careful customization in the zoning plan.

109495.explanation: Not a municipal task funded by all citizens through taxes.

109497.explanation: Arrange regionally. Does not necessarily have to be in Asten.

109502.explanation: There is already national policy in development.

109504.explanation: It is well organized in Asten. Hardly any complaints or nuisance.

109507.explanation: There is already a good arrangement for people who really need it, including sports vouchers.

109510.explanation: Children can join various music associations.

109511.explanation: Sports clubs maintain financially healthy policies independently of the municipality.

109513.explanation: We are already sufficiently well supplied.

109514.explanation: Entrepreneurs arrange matters themselves when necessary.

109521.explanation: There is already legislation that the municipality can use.

109523.explanation: Current arrangements offer good possibilities if needed.

109525.explanation: Citizens can arrange it themselves with energy companies.

109527.explanation: We have a generally good offering.

109530.explanation: Dog tax should be reduced to a much lower level: only to cover necessary costs. It can be drastically lowered.

109532.explanation: No large-scale sites are needed in Asten. But there should be provision especially for local businesses.

109534.explanation: They already receive priority within a national arrangement. We believe they should not have priority over our home seekers who have been on waiting lists for years.

109543.explanation: Three or four stories high, but fitting within a visual quality plan.

109544.explanation: This already happens with labor migrants.

109547.explanation: Destination traffic must always be kept to a minimum.

109549.explanation: We do hope Eindhoven will provide some reciprocal contribution.

109550.explanation: There is a statutory regulation for that.

109492.explanation: By residents contributing to the management of municipal facilities, the municipality can save costs. This translates into lower taxes. This is easier for maintenance of parks than for a sports hall.

109494.explanation: The municipality must ensure a balanced distribution of activities. Although there is room for more campsites, this does not mean a campsite should be allowed at every farm. We support opening (many) more farm shops with local products.

109495.explanation: The municipality of Asten should strive to be as energy-neutral as possible. Setting up an organization to jointly generate sustainable energy involves costs but contributes to sustainable energy goals. The municipality should therefore support these initiatives!

109497.explanation: We stand for humane reception of people fleeing war and violence. Small-scale shelter is better for integration and the wellbeing of asylum seekers and the neighborhood. Since nationally small-scale shelter has been chosen, Asten must contribute its part.

109501.explanation: At a time when we are more conscious of natural resources and switching to sustainable energy, heating the open air no longer fits with our municipal goals. Therefore, we switch to more sustainable solutions that also offer comfort without CO2 waste...

109502.explanation: Fireworks cause much nuisance and emissions of hazardous substances like fine particles and heavy metals. This pollution settles in our soil and surface waters. Organizing a central light show, for example, is possible if there is demand.

109504.explanation: Measures must be taken against hearing damage from prolonged exposure to loud noise; tightening noise standards can be part of this. At minimum, Asten must conform to the standards in the Hearing Damage Prevention Covenant.

109507.explanation: Sports are good for body and mind. It keeps your brain sharp for better concentration at school or work and is an important pillar for meeting each other. Membership of a sports club should be possible for everyone.

109509.explanation: The municipality of Asten has renovated almost all school buildings in recent years. Further improvement of all school buildings does not seem to be currently needed.

109510.explanation: Music lessons are good for children's development but unfortunately not affordable for every parent. In such cases, it is good to ensure through subsidies that every child can take music lessons. But this does not mean that individual music lessons for every child must be subsidized.

109511.explanation: Sports and exercise are important for body and mind, they foster social bonds and contribute to social participation. But that does not mean all sports clubs should automatically receive funding. Whether financial support is desirable should be assessed case by case.

109513.explanation: Feeling safe is a prerequisite for an attractive, livable village. People often feel less safe even if crime and nuisance figures do not show this. It must be determined whether there is a real increase in insecurity and if more community officers are needed.

109514.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to a feeling of safety but simultaneously infringes on people's privacy. At specific locations camera surveillance could be valuable but the pros and cons must be carefully weighed.

109521.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate and contribute to society according to their ability (including people on social assistance) and the municipality should encourage this where possible. But simply making “socially meaningful work” mandatory does not do justice to people’s personal circumstances.

109523.explanation: By raising the income threshold to 130%, we support 'working poor'. This group often falls through the cracks despite having higher fixed costs than disposable income. This prevents expensive debt trajectories later and ensures work pays off without losing all support.

109525.explanation: The number of electric cars will increase strongly in the coming years. Using private chargers, preferably with private solar panels, is preferred, also to balance the power grid and prevent congestion. But more public charging points will be needed.

109526.explanation: Lower speeds contribute to traffic safety and ensure fewer vehicles use village centers for through traffic. They also reduce nuisance (fine particles, exhaust gases, noise) and provide opportunities to make centers greener and more sustainable.

109527.explanation: We live in a pluralistic society and everyone is different. In a village like Asten it is not possible to have a healthcare provider available for every faith, culture or belief.

109528.explanation: Youth care places a heavy burden on the costs for a municipality. However, by focusing mainly on price, the quality of youth care will come under pressure and there will be more frequent incidents of misconduct. There must always be a balance between quality and costs, also when providing youth care.

109530.explanation: The revenues from the dog tax cover the costs made for facilities such as dog walking areas. Here too, the polluter (the owner) pays.

109532.explanation: Entrepreneurs will always want to grow and there will always be demand for more space for business parks. Expansion of companies should primarily take place through developments on existing business parks. This space can be used smarter and more effectively.

109534.explanation: Without priority, status holders will have to live for years in overcrowded asylum seeker centers (AZCs); they have had no opportunity to build waiting time and therefore always end up at the bottom of every list. Also, many initially have no job or assets, making housing in the private sector unaffordable.

109539.explanation: Every child should receive swimming lessons and obtain a swimming diploma; the municipality must ensure this is possible. We support school swimming, but the municipality of Asten cannot bear the costs itself, and school swimming also takes up a lot of lesson time.

109541.explanation: If an entrepreneur assesses that there is a need for a coffeeshop, this should be possible. If there is such a need, it means that now there is a lot of illegal soft drug trade taking place. This involves street dealing without age checks or security.

109543.explanation: Preferably, we realize more housing within the cores, but we want to preserve the village character of Asten. More high-rise buildings will be needed, but 'tall residential towers' refer to buildings with, for example, more than five floors. That does not fit the village character of Asten.

109544.explanation: If there is a need for room occupancy, this should be possible. By making this formally possible, rules can be established and illegal subletting can be prevented.

109546.explanation: The center of Asten must become car-restricted; parking can be on the edge of the center. Above all, it must be made attractive not to come to the center by car but to take the bicycle.

109547.explanation: PGA favors a car-free market in a car-restricted center. The center must be attractive for shopping, staying, and recreation. This cannot be the case if cars are everywhere parked and driving, even when you are sitting on the terrace. We must ensure that Asten is attractive for cycling.

109549.explanation: The new bus station in Eindhoven has limited added value for the residents of Asten, and we have already paid for it. Having a regional transport policy is good, but that cannot mean that improvements in public transport take place only directly around and in Eindhoven.

109550.explanation: It is becoming increasingly clear that pesticides also pose a risk to humans, animals, and the environment over a large radius around the places where they are used. Only the use of low-risk or organic products should be allowed.

109492.explanation: VVD Asten wants to stimulate social initiatives by residents and associations and interfere as little as possible with rules. If residents want to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or park, the municipality actively thinks along and provides support where appropriate.

109494.explanation: VVD Asten gives farmers space for side activities such as tourism or selling from home, but not without limits. Initiatives must fit the environment and take into account nature, livability, and support.

109495.explanation: VVD Asten supports sustainability and sees a role for the municipality as an independent advisor, for example through energy cooperatives. Structural financial support is not a given. Any subsidies are a task for the central government.

109497.explanation: VVD Asten does not choose to establish an asylum seekers center in our municipality. If an AZC is imposed under the distribution law, VVD will aim for the most favorable situation for the municipality of Asten, with safety for residents as the central focus.

109501.explanation: VVD Asten focuses on sustainability that remains affordable and gives entrepreneurs space. A general ban on terrace heating does not fit this. VVD chooses realistic measures and personal responsibility, not bans.

109502.explanation: VVD Asten values safety and livability. Balancing these requires tailor-made solutions and cost-awareness.

109504.explanation: VVD Asten focuses on a vibrant village with space for events and entrepreneurs. Stricter noise standards do not fit this. Nuisance must be prevented, but within reasonable and workable agreements, not with extra restrictions.

109507.explanation: VVD Asten finds sports important and contributes financially to children's sports when parents cannot afford it. For adults, no contribution applies.

109509.explanation: There is a clear housing plan for schools in the municipality of Asten. This is well underway.

109510.explanation: VVD Asten finds education and development through music, sports, and culture important and stimulates space for this in and around schools. There is no subsidy linked to this.

109511.explanation: VVD Asten sees sports clubs as important for health and cohesion and supports sports where needed. Additional financial support is not a given and always requires tailor-made solutions.

109513.explanation: VVD Asten states that additional community enforcement officers increase visibility of enforcement and help combat nuisance and minor crimes. Visible enforcement is important for a safe and pleasant Asten.

109514.explanation: VVD Asten supports the use of camera surveillance to increase safety and combat crime. Camera surveillance can, where necessary and proportionate, contribute to a safer feeling and more effective enforcement.

109521.explanation: VVD Asten states that those receiving social assistance benefits must participate according to their ability. A counter-performance in the form of socially meaningful work is part of this, so people remain active and contribute to society.

109523.explanation: VVD Asten focuses on targeted support and early debt assistance, emphasizing participation and personal responsibility. Tailored and goal-oriented help is paramount.

109525.explanation: VVD Asten promotes sustainability that is practical and affordable. More charging points for electric cars support the energy transition and make sustainable transport accessible, without extra obstacles for residents.

109526.explanation: VVD Asten focuses on traffic safety and good traffic flow. VVD chooses tailor-made solutions: where it is safer, 30 km/h can apply, but not everywhere.

109527.explanation: VVD Asten puts the resident at the center and values good, accessible care. Appropriate care is important, but availability and quality remain leading.

109528.explanation: VVD Asten states that care must be quickly available and of good quality, with the resident central. For youth care, quality and effectiveness are decisive. Price is important, but must never prevail over good care.

109530.explanation: The VVD stands for low taxes and a fair distribution of costs, based on 'the polluter pays'. The revenue from the dog tax must continue to be used to combat nuisance.

109532.explanation: VVD Asten wants to give entrepreneurs room to grow and focus on more jobs in the municipality. Sufficient and well-equipped business parks are needed for this, considering accessibility and good integration in the environment.

109534.explanation: VVD Asten wants residents to be able to live affordably in their own municipality and chooses local binding for scarce social rental housing.

109539.explanation: VVD Asten emphasizes the importance of sports, exercise, and health for children. A balance requires tailor-made solutions and financial substantiation.

109541.explanation: VVD Asten emphasizes safety, livability, and combating nuisance and crime. Allowing a coffeeshop does not fit this principle and does not contribute to a safe and pleasant living environment.

109543.explanation: The VVD wants to build enough homes and focuses on appropriate housing types per location. High-rise buildings are not a goal in themselves and require careful consideration of livability and surroundings.

109544.explanation: VVD Asten focuses on sufficient housing and flow. VVD chooses tailor-made solutions: new housing forms can help, provided they fit livability, safety, and the neighborhood.

109546.explanation: VVD Asten aims for an accessible and attractive center for residents and visitors. Sufficient parking is essential for livability and a strong local economy.

109547.explanation: VVD Asten values an accessible and attractive center for residents, visitors, and entrepreneurs. A fully car-free center does not fit here; good accessibility and parking options remain important.

109549.explanation: VVD Asten emphasizes the importance of good accessibility and strong regional cooperation within the Eindhoven Metropolitan Region. Investing in public transport hubs such as the bus station in Eindhoven contributes to accessibility for Asten residents.

109550.explanation: VVD Asten chooses a realistic and feasible nature policy with a balance between nature, housing, and business. A general ban on crop protection agents does not fit. The VVD opts for careful application within rules, not for local bans.

statements.title: The municipality should not invest money in the new bus station in Eindhoven.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Asten should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Asten.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
High-rise residential buildings should be allowed in Asten.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The center of Asten should become car-free.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: More business parks should be developed in Asten.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: More parking spaces should be created in the center.
Asten must ban the use of pesticides.
